cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
host   = sys.argv[1]
device = sys.argv[2]
for d, _, fs in os.walk(host):
  p = os.path.relpath(d, host)
  if p != '.' and p.startswith('.'):
    continue
  for f in fs:
    print os.path.join(p,f)
    subprocess.check_call(['/usr/bin/adb.1.0.35', 'push',
                           os.path.realpath(os.path.join(host, p, f)),
                           os.path.join(device, p, f)])
name: push [START_DIR]/skia/resources/* /sdcard/revenge_of_the_skiabot/resources

cmd:
import contextlib
import math
import socket
import sys
import time
import urllib2

HASHES_URL = 'https://storage.googleapis.com/skia-infra-gm/hash_files/gold-prod-hashes.txt'
RETRIES = 5
TIMEOUT = 60
WAIT_BASE = 15

socket.setdefaulttimeout(TIMEOUT)
for retry in range(RETRIES):
  try:
    with contextlib.closing(
        urllib2.urlopen(HASHES_URL, timeout=TIMEOUT)) as w:
      hashes = w.read()
      with open(sys.argv[1], 'w') as f:
        f.write(hashes)
        break
  except Exception as e:
    print 'Failed to get uninteresting hashes from %s:' % HASHES_URL
    print e
    if retry == RETRIES:
      raise
    waittime = WAIT_BASE * math.pow(2, retry)
    print 'Retry in %d seconds.' % waittime
    time.sleep(waittime)
name: get uninteresting hashes
~followup_annotations: @@@STEP_LOG_LINE@python.inline@    print 'Failed to get uninteresting hashes from %s:' % HASHES_URL@@@

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
ADB = sys.argv[1]
cpu = int(sys.argv[2])
gov = sys.argv[3]

log = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'root'])
# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'
print log
if 'cannot' in log:
  raise Exception('adb root failed')

subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'echo "%s" > '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % (gov, cpu)])
actual_gov = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'cat '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()
if actual_gov != gov:
  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))
name: Set CPU 0's governor to ondemand
~followup_annotations: @@@STEP_LOG_LINE@python.inline@# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'@@@

cmd:
import subprocess
import sys
bin_dir = sys.argv[1]
sh      = sys.argv[2]
subprocess.check_call(['/usr/bin/adb.1.0.35', 'shell', 'sh', bin_dir + sh])
try:
  sys.exit(int(subprocess.check_output(['/usr/bin/adb.1.0.35', 'shell', 'cat',
                                        bin_dir + 'rc'])))
except ValueError:
  print "Couldn't read the return code.  Probably killed for OOM."
  sys.exit(1)
name: dm

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
ADB = sys.argv[1]
cpu = int(sys.argv[2])
gov = sys.argv[3]

log = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'root'])
# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'
print log
if 'cannot' in log:
  raise Exception('adb root failed')

subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'echo "%s" > '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % (gov, cpu)])
actual_gov = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'cat '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()
if actual_gov != gov:
  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))
name: Set CPU 0's governor to ondemand (6)

                  % (actual_gov, gov))

import sys
bin_dir = sys.argv[1]
sh      = sys.argv[2]
subprocess.check_call(['/usr/bin/adb.1.0.35', 'shell', 'sh', bin_dir + sh])
try:
  sys.exit(int(subprocess.check_output(['/usr/bin/adb.1.0.35', 'shell', 'cat',
                                        bin_dir + 'rc'])))
except ValueError:
  print "Couldn't read the return code.  Probably killed for OOM."
  sys.exit(1)

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
ADB = sys.argv[1]
cpu = int(sys.argv[2])
gov = sys.argv[3]

log = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'root'])
# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'
print log
if 'cannot' in log:
  raise Exception('adb root failed')

subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'echo "%s" > '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % (gov, cpu)])
actual_gov = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'cat '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()
if actual_gov != gov:
  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))
name: Set CPU 0's governor to ondemand (22)

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
ADB = sys.argv[1]
cpu = int(sys.argv[2])
gov = sys.argv[3]

log = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'root'])
# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'
print log
if 'cannot' in log:
  raise Exception('adb root failed')

subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'echo "%s" > '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % (gov, cpu)])
actual_gov = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'cat '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()
if actual_gov != gov:
  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
ADB = sys.argv[1]
cpu = int(sys.argv[2])
gov = sys.argv[3]

log = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'root'])
# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'
print log
if 'cannot' in log:
  raise Exception('adb root failed')

subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'echo "%s" > '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % (gov, cpu)])
actual_gov = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'cat '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()
if actual_gov != gov:
  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))
name: Set CPU 0's governor to ondemand (49)

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
ADB = sys.argv[1]
cpu = int(sys.argv[2])
gov = sys.argv[3]

log = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'root'])
# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'
print log
if 'cannot' in log:
  raise Exception('adb root failed')

subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'echo "%s" > '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % (gov, cpu)])
actual_gov = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'cat '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()
if actual_gov != gov:
  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))
name: Set CPU 0's governor to ondemand (60)

cpu = int(sys.argv[2])

    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()

  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))

import sys
bin_dir = sys.argv[1]
sh      = sys.argv[2]
subprocess.check_call(['/usr/bin/adb.1.0.35', 'shell', 'sh', bin_dir + sh])
try:
  sys.exit(int(subprocess.check_output(['/usr/bin/adb.1.0.35', 'shell', 'cat',
                                        bin_dir + 'rc'])))
except ValueError:
  print "Couldn't read the return code.  Probably killed for OOM."
  sys.exit(1)

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
ADB = sys.argv[1]
cpu = int(sys.argv[2])
gov = sys.argv[3]

log = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'root'])
# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'
print log
if 'cannot' in log:
  raise Exception('adb root failed')

subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'echo "%s" > '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % (gov, cpu)])
actual_gov = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'cat '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()
if actual_gov != gov:
  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))
name: Set CPU 0's governor to ondemand (76)
~followup_annotations: @@@STEP_LOG_LINE@python.inline@# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'@@@

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
ADB = sys.argv[1]
cpu = int(sys.argv[2])
gov = sys.argv[3]

log = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'root'])
# check for message like 'adbd cannot run as root in production builds'
print log
if 'cannot' in log:
  raise Exception('adb root failed')

subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'echo "%s" > '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % (gov, cpu)])
actual_gov = subprocess.check_output([ADB, 'shell', 'cat '
    '/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu%d/cpufreq/scaling_governor' % cpu]).strip()
if actual_gov != gov:
  raise Exception('(actual, expected) (%s, %s)'
                  % (actual_gov, gov))
name: Set CPU 0's governor to ondemand (87)

cmd:
import os
import subprocess
import sys
out = sys.argv[1]
log = subprocess.check_output(['/usr/bin/adb.1.0.35', 'logcat', '-d'])
for line in log.split('\n'):
  tokens = line.split()
  if len(tokens) == 11 and tokens[-7] == 'F' and tokens[-3] == 'pc':
    addr, path = tokens[-2:]
    local = os.path.join(out, os.path.basename(path))
    if os.path.exists(local):
      sym = subprocess.check_output(['addr2line', '-Cfpe', local, addr])
      line = line.replace(addr, addr + ' ' + sym.strip())
  print line
name: dump log
~followup_annotations: @@@STEP_LOG_LINE@python.inline@  if len(tokens) == 11 and tokens[-7] == 'F' and tokens[-3] == 'pc':@@@
